workflowtools 0.0.13

Sync manifest citations to a sidecar BibTeX file

Source: R/manifest_bibtex.R
sync_manifest_to_bibtex.Rd

One-way, conservative writer. Reads the manifest and the existing (curated) references bib; for every manifest record whose citation is eligible, writes one BibTeX entry to out. Eligible means:

sync_manifest_to_bibtex(
  manifest = "data/input_manifest.json",
  out = "citations/data-sources.bib",
  references_bib = "citations/references.bib",
  prune = FALSE
)

Arguments

manifest

Path to the manifest JSON file.

out

Path to the sidecar .bib file to write. Created if missing.

references_bib

Path to the curated bibliography. Read for key deduplication only; never modified.

prune

Logical. When TRUE, removes any entries already in out whose keys are no longer in the manifest. When FALSE (default), the function fully regenerates out from the manifest each call, which has the same effect; the argument is reserved for a future incremental-merge mode.

Value

A list summarising the write: written (integer count), skipped_external (integer count), skipped_collision (character vector of keys that collided with references_bib), invisibly.

Details

  • The record has a citation field with a bibtex_key.

  • citation$external is not TRUE (records flagged external = TRUE live in the curated bib and are deliberately not re-exported).

  • The bibtex_key is not already present in references_bib (warns and skips on collision so the curated bib stays the source of truth for keys it owns).

Records are sorted by bibtex_key for stable diffs. The output file starts with a banner identifying it as auto-generated; do not edit it by hand.

Contents

Developed by Alex M Chubaty.
